title: Vultar Cataclysm
content:

|name=Vultar Cataclysm
|conflict=[[Third Great Schism]]<ref name="CSWE VultarCataclysm">{{CSWECite|III|314|Vultar Cataclysm}}</ref>
|date=[[4250 BBY]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

|outcome=*[[Mundicide|Destruction]] of the Vultar [[Star system/Legends|system]]<ref name="NEC" />
*[[Vultar Nebula]] created<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}}</ref>
|keyparties=[[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]<ref name="GH39">{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 39: The Third Great Schism}}</ref>
|participants=
}}
{{Quote|But Old Republic records chronicle the Vultar Cataclysm, an event in which the activation of an artifact known as the Cosmic Turbine destroyed that system.|Edited transcript of the debriefing of Doctor Insmot Bowen|<ref name="Author's Cut" />}}
The '''Vultar Cataclysm''' was the [[Mundicide|destruction]] of the [[Vultar system/Legends|Vultar system]] in [[4250 BBY]]. During the [[Third Great Schism]] that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], a group of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] took up residence in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]] and discovered [[Celestial/Legends|Celestial]] machines there, including the [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]-manipulation device known as the [[Cosmic Turbine]]. The [[darksider]]s activated the machinery but were unable to control it, and as a result, the entire Vultar system was destroyed. [[3997 BBY|Centuries later]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Shayoto]] recalled the Vultar Cataclysm, and the event was also recorded in several historical documents.

==Prelude==
In [[4250 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> after a group of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] were driven from<ref name="GH39" /> the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] as part of the [[Third Great Schism]], they took up residence in the [[Vultar system/Legends|Vultar system]]. The adherents of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] discovered a set of massive machines there, including<ref name="GH39" /> the [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]-manipulation device<ref name="TEA" /> they dubbed the [[Cosmic Turbine]], that had been left behind by the ancient [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Celestial/Legends|Celestials]]<ref name="GH39" /> before they had disappeared from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] by [[30,000 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

==The cataclysm==
{{Quote|During the Vultar Cataclysm in the Third Great Schism, dark siders influenced by the Sith triggered the obliteration of an entire world.|Chief Librarian Restelly Quist, ''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force''|<ref name="JP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>}}
Seeking revenge against their [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] foes<ref name="GH39" /> and intending to unleash as much destruction as possible,<ref name="CSWE Timeline">{{CSWECite|I|xii|A Timeline of Galactic Events}}</ref> the Dark Jedi activated the Cosmic Turbine and the rest of the devices but proved unable to control them. Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] into the [[darksider]]s' attempt to use the Celestial machinery, a catastrophic accident<ref name="GH39" /> that came to be known as the Vultar Cataclysm<ref name="NEC" /> [[Mundicide|destroyed]] the entire Vultar [[Star system/Legends|system]]—including the devices and the Dark Jedi<ref name="GH39" />—leaving behind the [[Vultar Nebula]].<ref name="Author's Cut" /> The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] helped the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] deal with the catastrophe,<ref name="FF75">{{FactFile|75|JED 1-4|The Jedi}}</ref> and the event was witnessed by the long-lived Jedi [[Shayoto]].<ref name="DLotS3">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]]</ref>

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|The primary accounts of the Vultar Cataclysm would have been part of the Jedi Archives, which are restricted.|Edited transcript of the debriefing of Doctor Insmot Bowen|<ref name="Author's Cut" />}}
[[File:Shayoto.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Centuries after the Vultar Cataclysm, Shayoto still remembered the event.]]
The Vultar Cataclysm was eventually chronicled in Republic records.<ref name="Author's Cut" /> In [[3997 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> during the Jedi [[Conclave at Deneba|conclave]] on the planet [[Deneba/Legends|Deneba]] that sought to address the growing threat of the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] cult of darksiders in the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]],<ref name="DLotS3" /> the [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]]-old<ref name="ShayotoAge">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]], during which [[Shayoto]] mentions he was alive during the [[Gank Massacres]], to [[3997 BBY]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the latter event to [[4800 BBY]], therefore Shayoto must have been more than eight [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] old by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of ''Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3.</ref> Shayoto addressed the gathering, rhetorically inquiring whether anyone beside him remembered the Vultar Cataclysm before relating the methodologies of ancient organized dark side followers.<ref name="DLotS3" />

A section of the<ref name="JP" /> [[990 BBY]]<ref name="990 BBY">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2010/12/endnotes-for-star-wars-jedi-path-part-1.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The Jedi Path (part 1 of 4)|quote=Okay, so we've established that this particular copy of the [[Book/Legends|book]] dates from [[115 BBY]]. But the content inside is [...] dating from the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[990 BBY]].}}</ref> [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi trainee]] [[Book/Legends|guidebook]] ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]'' [[Author/Legends|authored]] by the Jedi [[Chief Librarian/Legends|Chief Librarian]] [[Restelly Quist/Legends|Restelly Quist]] noted the event,<ref name="JP" /> as did the [[Jedi historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]]'s work ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]'', published in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], the [[Obroan Institute for Archaeology]] researcher [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Insmot Bowen]] also mentioned the Vultar Cataclysm during a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] debriefing on the Celestials. The scholar hypothesized that the primary accounts of the event had been kept in the Jedi Order's [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Archives]], access to which the Empire had restricted.<ref name="Author's Cut" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Tojdls3cover.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Vultar Cataclysm was introduced in ''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3.]]
The Vultar Cataclysm was first mentioned in the [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|third issue]] of [[Dark Horse Comics]]' [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - Dark Lords of the Sith'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] mini-series. The issue was authored by [[Tom Veitch]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]]<ref name="DLotS3" /> and published on [[December 13]], [[1994]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54573/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_dark_lords_of_the_sith_1994_3|text=Star Wars: Tales Of The Jedi - Dark Lords Of The Sith (1994) #3}}</ref> In the [[1997]] [[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|audio dramatization]] of ''Dark Lords of the Sith'', Shayoto's line mentioning the event is erroneously delivered by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]] instead.<ref name="DLotSaudio">[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]]</ref> In addition, the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 116|116th issue]] of [[De Agostini]]'s ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' magazine,<ref name="FF116">{{FactFile|116|DOO 5-8|Count Dooku}}</ref> written by [[Iain Lowson]]<ref name="FF Author">{{LinkedIn|url=in/iainlowson|text=Iain Lowson|archivefile=File:IainLowson-LinkedIn.png}}</ref> and published around [[March 17]], [[2004]],<ref name="FF16Date">{{FFdates|116}}</ref> erroneously described the Vultar Cataclysm<ref name="FF116" />—as opposed to the Third Great Schism it was a part of<ref name="GH39" />—as a schism within the Jedi Order.<ref name="FF116" />

The Vultar Cataclysm was originally meant to be mentioned in the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]]; however, the section that would have mentioned the event was cut before the book's release. The information regarding the Vultar Cataclysm was later released in [[2013]] as part of [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut]], a series of articles published on the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars Blog|Blog]] by Fry and [[Del Rey]] editor [[Erich Schoeneweiss]] containing various cut content from ''The Essential Guide to Warfare''.<ref name="Author's Cut" />
